perturbation.original_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier shall be required to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier may, at its sole discretion, purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses 'shall be required,' establishing a mandatory purchase obligation for Premier. Changing this to 'may, at its sole discretion' introduces a contradiction. This shifts the obligation to a voluntary option, conflicting with the initial mandatory requirement. Premier now has the option to buy units but is not obligated to do so. This in-text contradiction undermines the intended enforcement of order quantities. This also impacts the text in the same section where the amount to be purchased is given, but due to the introduction of the changes, it becomes unclear, wether the amount should be purchased or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby agree to remove Section 2(c) in its entirety and replace it with the following: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier may, at its sole discretion, purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The MAOV [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, contingent upon commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019. For the avoidance of doubt, the six-month period commencing July 1, 2022 and ending December 31, 2022, Premier will be required to purchase [***] (the twelve-month periods and the six-month period are each a "Contract Period").
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated Premier to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume (MAOV), using the language 'shall be required.' The changed text replaces this with 'may, at its sole discretion,' transforming the mandatory obligation into an optional one. The contradicted text repeats the new optional wording, but the underlying MAOV concept still implies an expected minimum purchase amount. However, the new text gives Premier complete discretion whether to purchase this amount, creating a contradiction with the concept of MAOV amount provided as a minimum target. This contradiction creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding the MAOV requirements for Premier.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement may be subject to change based on mutual discussion.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that all other terms remain in full force and effect, providing certainty and stability to the contract. By changing it to 'may be subject to change based on mutual discussion', we create uncertainty about the rest of the agreement, and make it contradictory to the point where the original agreement is in full effect. The in-text contradiction arises because it is not clear what are the exact remaining terms and conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement may be subject to change based on mutual discussion. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have caused this Amendment to be signed by their respective duly authorized representatives as of the Amendment Effective Date. Premier Nutrition Corporation Stremick's Heritage Foods, LLC. By: /s/ Darcy Davenport By: /s/ Sam Stremick Name: Darcy Davenport Name: Sam Stremick Title: President Title: President Source: BELLRING BRANDS, INC., S-1, 9/20/2019
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect,' indicating a stable and unchanged agreement. However, the changed text modifies this to 'all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement may be subject to change based on mutual discussion.' This creates a direct contradiction, as the original agreement is set to remain the same, but the changed text introduces uncertainty and the possibility of alteration. The Contradicted Text is the Changed text, because this change modifies a previously set condition, thus triggering an in-text contradiction.
